summary: Implemented the curmudgeon SEC-8b.c1 hole #12 three-part edit for 8b.3 L3a non-discrimination. Patch replaces the entire <details id="p8b-3-eclipse-layers"> block in data/sections.json -> part8b.html with new replacement HTML that: (1) adds explicit 'A Pass in this band does not distinguish dome from globe' sentence to the L3a body, cross-linking WIN-068's code_analysis tag derives_from_dome:false as the architectural discriminator; (2) extends the (d) Unchanged clause from one item to two, with the second item making the L3a non-discrimination critique parallel to the ensemble-anchor critique; (3) changes the adoption-label from 'ADOPTED (FULL)' to 'ADOPTED (FULL as steel-man)', mirroring 8b.2's existing label. Also promotes the non-discrimination critique to the ps-tldr so a skim-reader sees it before expanding (curmudgeon's optional follow-on), and surfaces the Fail-clause logical tension (SQ variation cannot be a separable alternative when Chapman's mechanism IS the SQ-dynamo). Word delta +238. One note for decider: the ISS-1190 description references 'WIN-068 Exhibit A forensic analysis' — WIN-068 itself does not contain an 'Exhibit A' (Exhibit A is tied to WIN-058/WIN-058b's κ coupling claim); the architectural discriminator the curmudgeon's recommendation actually cites is WIN-068's code_analysis tag derives_from_dome:false. The expansion uses that correct anchor. Recommend closing ISS-1190 after integration.
